response:
I will overlay the existing cell coordinates on the H&E image, as your data is already processed at the single-cell level (160,286 segmented cells).

```python
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Get slice 0
slice_0 = session.get_slice(0)

# Check if H&E image is available
if 'he' in slice_0.images:
    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 12))
    
    # Display the H&E image
    plt.imshow(slice_0.images['he'])
    
    # Overlay the already segmented cell centroids
    plt.scatter(slice_0.adata.obs['x'], slice_0.adata.obs['y'], 
                s=0.5, c='red', alpha=0.6, label='Cell Centroids')
    
    plt.title('Segmented Cells Overlaid on H&E Image')
    plt.legend()
    plt.axis('off')
    plt.show()
else:
    print("H&E image is not available for this slice. Plotting spatial coordinates only.")
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
    plt.scatter(slice_0.adata.obs['x'], slice_0.adata.obs['y'], 
                s=0.5, c='black', alpha=0.5)
    plt.title('Spatial Distribution of Segmented Cells')
    plt.axis('equal')
    plt.show()
```

```
H&E image is not available for this slice. Plotting spatial coordinates only.

```
